repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

comment_data.body: Note that this drops “and it has not been changed inside of Vim” wording. Could be unwise, we're now implying that it is read in all cases. Minor though.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -301,12 +301,13 @@ local options = {
       abbreviation = 'ar',
       defaults = true,
       desc = [=[
-        When a file has been detected to have been changed outside of Vim and
-        it has not been changed inside of Vim, automatically read it again.
-        When the file has been deleted this is not done, so you have the text
-        from before it was deleted.  When it appears again then it is read.
-        Nvim uses file system watchers to detect changes in real-time for all
-        loaded buffers; see |timestamp| for details.
+        When a file was changed outside of Nvim, automatically read it again.

comment_data.body: If we want to deduplicate even more, these retrys with assertions can probably be extracted into a helper function.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -41,47 +44,53 @@ describe('autoread file watcher', function()
     eq({ 'startup original' }, api.nvim_buf_get_lines(0, 0, -1, true))
     eq(true, is_watching())
 
-    -- Modify file externally; watcher should already be active.
     write_file(path, 'startup changed\n')
-
     retry(nil, 3000, function()
       eq({ 'startup changed' }, api.nvim_buf_get_lines(0, 0, -1, true))
     end)
   end)
 
-  it('reloads buffer when file changes externally', function()
-    local path = testdir .. '/test_reload.txt'
-    write_file(path, 'original content\n')
-
-    command('edit ' .. path)
-    eq({ 'original content' }, api.nvim_buf_get_lines(0, 0, -1, true))
-    eq(true, is_watching())
+  it('reloads on external change; survives hide; undoable; bdelete stops watch', function()
+    local path = open_watched('original content\n')
+    local bufnr = api.nvim_get_current_buf()
 
-    -- Modify file externally
+    -- 1. Plain external change reloads the visible buffer.
     write_file(path, 'new content\n')
-
-    -- The watcher + debounce should trigger checktime
     retry(nil, 3000, function()

comment_data.body: Note that today `neovim` does not have a consistent naming convention for local constants. some are `like_this`, some are `LIKE_THIS`. Could be worth it to do a global sweep (and perhaps codify in a doc).
Note that there is already a private `_is_watching` below - perhaps it would make sense to visually group them nearby.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -14,7 +14,14 @@ local watchers = {}
 --- @type table<integer, uv.uv_timer_t> bufnr -> debounce timer
 local timers = {}
 
-local DEBOUNCE_MS = 100
+local debounce_ms = 100
+
+--- @private
+--- Test-only: override the debounce window so tests can run faster.
+--- @param ms integer
+function M._set_debounce(ms)

comment_data.body: Should also probably be next to `is_watching` above (that also reaches into module internals). Note that right now these two functions use different style when executing Lua code within - can be made more consistent.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -27,9 +27,15 @@ local function open_watched(content)
   return path
 end
 
+--- Shortens the 'autoread' debounce window so each test doesn't pay the 100ms time-cost.
+local function shorten_debounce()

comment_data.body: This comment is now misleading - since debounce interval has been shortened to 10ms.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -132,25 +139,32 @@ describe('autoread file watcher', function()
     eq({ 'will be deleted' }, api.nvim_buf_get_lines(0, 0, -1, true))
   end)
 
-  -- TODO: revisit this test
-  it('handles rapid changes with debouncing', function()
-    local path = testdir .. '/test_debounce.txt'
-    write_file(path, 'v1\n')
+  it('coalesces rapid changes via debouncing', function()
+    local path = open_watched('v1\n')
 
-    command('edit ' .. path)
-    eq({ 'v1' }, api.nvim_buf_get_lines(0, 0, -1, true))
-    eq(true, is_watching())
+    -- Count buffer reloads triggered by the watcher.
+    n.exec_lua([[
+      _G.reloads = 0
+      vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd('FileChangedShellPost', {
+        callback = function() _G.reloads = _G.reloads + 1 end,
+      })
+    ]])
 
-    -- Make several rapid changes
+    -- 4 back-to-back writes well inside one 100ms debounce window.

comment_data.body: If the goal of this commit was to truly ensure debouncing logic, counting buffer reloads might not accomplish that goal. Let's say that we remove debounce, and OS event(s) were delivered asynchronously - the first check would then reload, and the subsequent ones will be no-op. So the test would succeed despite no debounce occurring.

@@ -132,25 +139,32 @@ describe('autoread file watcher', function()
     eq({ 'will be deleted' }, api.nvim_buf_get_lines(0, 0, -1, true))
   end)

-  it('handles rapid changes with debouncing', function()

-    write_file(path, 'v1\n')
+  it('coalesces rapid changes via debouncing', function()

-    command('edit ' .. path)
-    eq({ 'v1' }, api.nvim_buf_get_lines(0, 0, -1, true))
-    eq(true, is_watching())
+    -- Count buffer reloads triggered by the watcher.

comment_data.body:
Thanks, yes, that was intentional because even if it's changed, we try to autoread and show the W12 warning as necessary. 

Also given that there is a prompt, it's not worth the awkward wording in the doc.

comment_data.body: yes. i would prefer lowercase, but regardless, this is no longer even a "constant" because we actually do set it.
moved `_is_watching` up, since its placement seemed a bit random
